title: migrate to go modules
body:
Note that the CNI_COMMIT in the Makefile now evaluates to something
looking like that: v0.0.0-20190506202554-9467ac9cfd92.  The reason
for the format change is because the `go.mod` file is the only source
of truth and go simply stores certain version information like that.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body: The time has come :smiling_imp: Once this PR is in, we can use dependabot for opening PRs to update dependencies.

Looks like a dependency is messed up somewhere.

```
go: finding github.com/mvdan/interfacer latest
can't load package: package github.com/mvdan/interfacer/cmd/interfacer: unknown import path "github.com/mvdan/interfacer/cmd/interfacer": cannot find module providing package github.com/mvdan/interfacer/cmd/interfacer
WARNING: failed to install github.com/mvdan/interfacer/cmd/interfacer: exit status 1
```

There's some more work needed before we can use go modules (mostly CI-related). I'd love to get https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint into the CI as we already did for CRI-O. The metalinter one seems to be abandoned.
@vrothberg Any movement on this?

> @vrothberg Any movement on this?

Nope. We need `golangci-lint` in before.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 4ae0a69) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably c22957b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @edsantiago @saschagrunert PTAL
Splitting the build and test dependencies into separate modules could also be done for libpod (and cri-o).
@TomSweeneyRedHat, CI is finally green and we're back on gometalinter. The PR can trivially be rebased now once we're ready for the migration.

> About ten more, but I'll hold off on entering those unless they're actually a problem (I'm guessing they're not).

The source of truth is the `go.mod` file. The `go.sum` one is containing hashes of currently and previously used versions/revisions, so we can safely ignore it.

I just deleted `go.sum` and re-ran `make vendor` which resulted in the following diff:

Thanks a lot for checking btw, @edsantiago :pray:

@vrothberg thanks for your explanation. Comparing vendor.conf and go.mod, and ignoring the many newly-added packages and handful of removed ones, the only diff that stands out to me is:
```
github.com/opencontainers/selinux: vendor=v1.1 gomod=v0.0.0-20190118194635-b707dfcb00a1
```
If I understand go modules correctly, `v1` is an important indicator of stability. Can I assume that your choice to use a `v0` here is intentional? And would it merit a git commit message (or comment in go.mod)?

This is very minor and should not in any block you from merging.

> @vrothberg thanks for your explanation. Comparing vendor.conf and go.mod, and ignoring the many newly-added packages and handful of removed ones, the only diff that stands out to me is:
> 
> ```
> github.com/opencontainers/selinux: vendor=v1.1 gomod=v0.0.0-20190118194635-b707dfcb00a1
> ```
> 
> If I understand go modules correctly, `v1` is an important indicator of stability. Can I assume that your choice to use a `v0` here is intentional? And would it merit a git commit message (or comment in go.mod)?

Awesome catch, @edsantiago! I fixed it by moving to selinux v1.2.2. Not sure how it ended up like that.

Thank you. The rest of the changes look OK to me, but I'll include them below for your review anyway:
```

  gopkg.in/fsnotify.v1
  gopkg.in/gemnasium/logrus-airbrake-hook.v2
  gopkg.in/tomb.v1
  gotest.tools
Diff:
  github.com/docker/go-units                           v0.3.2 -> v0.3.3
  github.com/docker/libnetwork           1a06131fb8a047d919f7 -> v0.8.0-dev.2.0.20180608203834-19279f049241
  github.com/opencontainers/go-digest    c9281466c8b2f606084a -> v1.0.0-rc1

  github.com/opencontainers/selinux                      v1.1 -> v1.2.2
  github.com/seccomp/containers-golang                   v0.1 -> v0.0.0-20180629143253-cdfdaa7543f4
  golang.org/x/sys                       7fbe1cd0fcc20051e1fc -> v0.0.0-20190422165155-953cdadca894
  k8s.io/client-go                       kubernetes-1.10.13-b -> v0.0.0-20181219152756-3dd551c0f083
```

I restarted the one flaked Travis job.

> Thank you. The rest of the changes look OK to me, but I'll include them below for your review anyway:

I think we need to compare the `./vendor` tree as well. `go-ansiterm`, for instance, is not mentioned in `go.mod` but in `go.sum` and it's in `./vendor`. To be honest, I don't yet know why...

Chained dependency? It's listed in `vendor/github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/go.mod`. IIRC that's one of the rationales behind go modules, the ability to bring in chained dependencies without API breaks.
@edsantiago, it looks like it's one from Windows :^)
Tests are green. LGTM. This was a doozy, thanks for your hard work on it.
Need WIP label removed.

> Need WIP label removed.

Done :heavy_check_mark: I will prepare follow-up PRs to update the dependencies once this one's merged.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
